title: Fix the factorial function
explanation: The base case returns 0 instead of 1, making all factorial results multiply by 0.
hint: Check the base case of your recursion. What value should factorial(0) return to avoid multiplying everything by zero?

title: Fix the even number checker
buggy_code:
def is_even(n):
    return n % 2 != 0
correct_code:
def is_even(n):
    return n % 2 == 0
explanation: Uses != instead of == when checking remainder, inverting the result.
hint: Look closely at the condition in your return statement. Is it checking for equality or inequality?

title: Fix the list sum function
explanation: Range starts at 1 instead of 0, skipping the first element in the sum.
hint: Check the starting index of your loop. Are you including every element of the list, or starting one step too late?

title: Fix the max finder
explanation: Initializing max to 0 fails for all-negative lists since 0 is greater than every element.
hint: Consider what happens if the list only contains negative numbers. Is 0 a safe starting value for find_max?

title: Fix the string reversal
buggy_code:
def reverse_string(s):
    return s[::1]
correct_code:
def reverse_string(s):
    return s[::-1]
explanation: Slice step should be -1 to reverse, not 1 which returns the original string.
hint: Look at the step parameter in your string slice. What value iterates through the string in reverse?

title: Fix the Fibonacci function
explanation: Returns variable 'a' instead of 'b', giving the previous Fibonacci number.
hint: Check which variable holds the most recently calculated Fibonacci number at the end of the loop.

title: Fix the duplicate remover
explanation: Condition is inverted - adds items already seen instead of items not yet seen.
hint: Look at the logic that decides whether to add an item to the result. Should it happen if the item is already seen, or if it isn't?

title: Fix the power calculator
explanation: Uses += (addition) instead of *= (multiplication), computing base*exp+1 instead of base^exp.
hint: Consider the mathematical operator inside your loop. Is it performing addition or multiplication?

title: Fix the vowel counter
explanation: Increments count by 2 instead of 1 for each vowel, doubling the result.
hint: Check the increment value in your counter. For each vowel found, how much should the total increase by?

title: Fix the average calculator
explanation: Uses integer division (//) instead of float division (/), truncating decimal results.
hint: Look at the type of division you are using. Does it allow for decimal results, or does it truncate them?

title: Fix the binary search
explanation: Loop condition uses < instead of <=, missing the target when low equals high.
hint: Think about the loop condition. Does it stop before or after checking the very last possible element?

title: Fix the insertion sort
explanation: Places key at arr[j] instead of arr[j+1] after the while loop decrements j past the insertion point.
hint: Consider where the key should be placed once the correct position is found. Observe the final value of j.

title: Fix the matrix transpose
explanation: Reads from matrix[j][i] instead of matrix[i][j], failing to swap row and column indices.
hint: Look at the indices used to access the original matrix. Are you pulling the value from the row and column you expect?

title: Fix the array sum
explanation: Sum is initialized to 1 instead of 0, adding an extra 1 to the result.
hint: Check the initial value of your sum variable. Does it start at a neutral value for addition?

title: Fix the average calculator
explanation: Integer division truncates the decimal. Must cast to float before dividing.
hint: Think about the data types involved in your division. How can you ensure the result is a floating-point number?

title: Fix the multiplication table
explanation: Uses addition (n + i) instead of multiplication (n * i) for the times table.
hint: Check the operation used to generate the values. Is it calculating a sum or a product for the multiplication table?

title: Fix the min finder
explanation: Uses > instead of < in comparison, finding maximum instead of minimum.
hint: Look at the comparison in your if-statement. Is it searching for the smallest value or the largest?

title: Fix the power of two checker
explanation: Returns 0 at the end instead of 1, always reporting false even for valid powers of two.
hint: What should the function return if it successfully passes through the while loop without returning 0?

title: Fix the bubble sort
explanation: Swap assigns arr[j+1] = arr[j] instead of arr[j+1] = temp, breaking the swap operation.
hint: Check the triple-assignment logic used for swapping. Is the temp variable being used correctly to hold the original value?

title: Fix the string reverse
explanation: Uses index len-i instead of len-1-i, accessing the null terminator and producing wrong results.
hint: Remember that string indices are 0-based. If a string has length 5, what is the index of the very last character?

title: Fix the prime checker
explanation: Returns 1 when a divisor is found instead of 0, inverting the prime check logic.
hint: Think about what it means if n % i is 0. Should that return true or false for the prime check?

title: Fix the GCD function
explanation: Assigns a = b instead of a = temp, losing the original value of b needed for the algorithm.
hint: Look at how you are updating variable 'a' in the Euclidean algorithm. Which previous value should it inherit?

title: Fix the digit counter
explanation: Divides by 100 instead of 10, skipping digits and undercounting.
hint: Check the reduction step in your loop. How many digits are you stripping off n in each iteration?

title: Fix the binary to decimal converter
explanation: Multiplies base by 10 instead of 2, treating input as decimal rather than binary.
hint: Consider the base of the number system you are converting *to*. Should it increase by powers of 10 or 2?

title: Fix the selection sort
explanation: Swap assigns arr[min_idx] = arr[i] instead of arr[min_idx] = temp, breaking the swap.
hint: Verify the swap logic at the end of the outer loop. Is the value in 'temp' actually being used to update 'arr[min_idx]'?

title: Fix the vector sum
explanation: Uses <= instead of < in loop condition, causing out-of-bounds access.
hint: Check your loop's upper bound. In a vector of size N, what is the highest valid index you can access?

title: Fix the square calculator
explanation: Uses addition (n + n) instead of multiplication (n * n), computing 2n instead of n^2.
hint: Look at the math in your return statement. Is it doubling the number or squaring it?

title: Fix the string length counter
explanation: Counter starts at 1 instead of 0, making the result always one more than actual length.
hint: What should the length count be before you start counting individual characters?

title: Fix the sign checker
explanation: Second condition checks n > 0 instead of n < 0, making negatives fall through to zero.
hint: Look closely at the conditions for positive and negative checks. Are they distinct?

title: Fix the array copy
explanation: Always copies src[0] instead of src[i], filling destination with only the first element.
hint: Check which index of the source array you are accessing inside the loop. Does it change with each iteration?

title: Fix the FizzBuzz
explanation: Fizz and Buzz labels are swapped - Fizz should be for multiples of 3, Buzz for multiples of 5.
hint: Check the return values for 3 and 5. Which word is traditionally associated with which number in FizzBuzz?

title: Fix the recursive sum
explanation: Subtracts 2 instead of 1 in the recursive call, skipping every other number.
hint: Look at the argument passed to the recursive call. How much is the value of n decreasing by each time?

title: Fix the vowel remover
explanation: Keeps vowels instead of removing them by using != instead of == in the condition.
hint: Check the condition in your if-statement. Are you adding the character to the result if it IS a vowel, or if it ISN'T?

title: Fix the array rotation
explanation: Places the saved first element back at index 0 instead of the last position (n-1).
hint: Where should the very first element be moved during a left rotationâ€”the front or the back?

title: Fix the digit sum
explanation: Divides by 100 instead of 10, processing only every other digit.
hint: Look at the reduction step of your loop. Are you processing every digit, or skipping every other one?

title: Fix the merge sorted arrays
explanation: Last while loop checks j < a.size() instead of j < b.size(), not appending remaining elements from b.
hint: Verify the loop condition for the second while loop. Is it checking the index against the correct vector's size?

title: Fix the second largest finder
explanation: Missing 'second = first' before updating first, so second never captures the previous maximum.
hint: When you find a new largest number, what should happen to the value that was previously the largest?

title: Fix the palindrome number check
explanation: Returns reversed != original instead of reversed == original, inverting the palindrome result.
hint: Look at the final comparison. Should it return true if the numbers match or if they don't match?

title: Fix the factorial calculator
explanation: Base case returns 0 instead of 1 for factorial(0).
hint: Check the return value for the base case (n == 0). What is the mathematical definition of 0 factorial?

title: Fix the array reverse
explanation: Uses index n-i instead of n-1-i, causing ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
hint: Remember that array indices are zero-based. If an array has 5 elements, what is the index of the last element?

title: Fix the string comparison
explanation: Uses == to compare String objects (reference equality) instead of .equals() for value equality.
hint: Think about how Java compares objects vs primitives. How do you check if the contents of two strings are equal?

title: Fix the sum calculator
explanation: Loop uses < instead of <=, excluding n from the sum (computes 1+2+3+4=10 instead of 15).
hint: Check the loop boundary. Are you including the final number 'n' in your cumulative sum?

title: Fix the absolute value
explanation: Returns n instead of -n when negative, failing to negate the value.
hint: Look at the logic for negative numbers. Should the return value still have a negative sign?

title: Fix the palindrome checker
explanation: Initializes right to s.length() instead of s.length()-1, causing StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.
hint: Check the initial value of your 'right' pointer. If a string has length L, what is the index of its last character?

title: Fix the number reverser
explanation: Uses n %= 10 instead of n /= 10, creating an infinite loop instead of removing the last digit.
hint: Look at the operation used to reduce 'n'. Are you removing the last digit, or just finding the remainder?

title: Fix the max of three
explanation: Returns a instead of c in the final return, never returning the third value when it is the largest.
hint: Check the final return statement. What happens if the third variable 'c' is the largest of the three?

title: Fix the Armstrong checker
explanation: Uses digits-1 as the exponent instead of digits, computing the wrong power sum.
hint: Consider the formula for Armstrong numbers. Should each digit be raised to the power of (number of digits) or (number of digits - 1)?

title: Fix the switch statement
explanation: Cases 6 and 7 return 'weekday' instead of 'weekend'.
hint: Check the return values for case 6 and 7. Should Saturday and Sunday be considered weekdays?

title: Fix the binary search
explanation: Sets low = mid instead of low = mid + 1, causing an infinite loop when target is in the upper half.
hint: Look at how 'low' is updated when the target is larger than the midpoint. Could this lead to an infinite loop?

title: Fix the matrix diagonal sum
explanation: Uses n-i instead of n-1-i for anti-diagonal index, causing ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
hint: Verify the indices used for the anti-diagonal. Does 'n - i' point to a valid index when i is 0?

title: Fix the sum of list
explanation: Subtracts instead of adding.
hint: Check the operator inside the loop.

title: Fix the even checker
explanation: Inverted condition.
hint: Even numbers have remainder 0 when divided by 2.

title: Fix the string reverse
buggy_code:
def reverse_string(s):
    return s[::-2]
correct_code:
def reverse_string(s):
    return s[::-1]
explanation: Step -2 instead of -1.
hint: Check the slicing step.

title: Fix max finder
buggy_code:
def f(a,b,c):
 if a>b and a>c: return a
 elif b>a and b>a: return b
 return a
correct_code:
def f(a,b,c):
 if a>=b and a>=c: return a
 elif b>=a and b>=c: return b
 return c
explanation: Logic error; returns a instead of c.
hint: What if c is the largest?

title: Fix list multiply
explanation: Adds instead of multiplying.
hint: Multiply, don't add.

title: Fix range sum
buggy_code:
def f(n):
 s=0
 for i in range(n): s+=i
 return s
correct_code:
def f(n):
 s=0
 for i in range(1, n+1): s+=i
 return s
explanation: Range excludes n.
hint: Does range(n) include n?

title: Fix square list
buggy_code:
def f(l):
 return [x*2 for x in l]
correct_code:
def f(l):
 return [x**2 for x in l]
explanation: Multiplies by 2 instead of squaring.
hint: Square operator is **.

title: Fix join string
buggy_code: def f(l): return ','.join(l)
correct_code: def f(l): return ' '.join(l)
explanation: Joins with comma instead of space.
hint: Use space separator.

title: Fix the index error
buggy_code:
def get_last(lst):
    return lst[len(lst)]
correct_code:
def get_last(lst):
    return lst[len(lst) - 1]
explanation: Lists are 0-indexed, so len(lst) is out of bounds.
hint: Remember that the last valid index of a list is its length minus one.

title: Fix lambda sort
buggy_code: def f(l): return sorted(l, key=lambda x: x[0])
correct_code: def f(l): return sorted(l, key=lambda x: x[1])
explanation: Sorts by first element instead of second.
hint: Check the index in lambda.

title: Fix filter evens
buggy_code: def f(l): return [x for x in l if x%2==1]
correct_code: def f(l): return [x for x in l if x%2==0]
explanation: Filters odds instead of evens.
hint: x%2==0 for evens.

title: Fix string capitalize
buggy_code: def f(s): return s.title()
correct_code: def f(s): return s.capitalize()
explanation: title() caps every word, capitalize() caps only first.
hint: Only first letter should be caps.

title: Fix set difference
buggy_code: def f(a,b): return a | b
correct_code: def f(a,b): return a - b
explanation: Union instead of difference.
hint: Use subtraction for difference.

title: Fix deep copy
buggy_code: def f(l): return l[:]
correct_code:
import copy
def f(l): return copy.deepcopy(l)
explanation: Shallow copy instead of deep copy.
hint: Nested lists need deepcopy.

title: Fix class count
explanation: Sets counter to 1 instead of incrementing.
hint: Increment the class variable.

title: Fix generator limit
explanation: Excludes the limit n.
hint: Should be inclusive of n.

title: Fix list index
explanation: Returns value instead of index.
hint: We need the position, not the data.

title: Fix vowel check
buggy_code: int is_v(char c){ if(c=='a'||c=='p') return 1; return 0; }
correct_code: int is_v(char c){ if(c=='a'||c=='e') return 1; return 0; }
explanation: Checks 'p' instead of 'e'.
hint: Check vowels.

title: Fix even check
buggy_code: int f(int n){ return n%2==1; }
correct_code: int f(int n){ return n%2==0; }
explanation: Returns 1 for odd.
hint: Even is n%2==0.

title: Fix max2
buggy_code: int f(int a,int b){ if(a<b) return a; return b; }
correct_code: int f(int a,int b){ if(a>b) return a; return b; }
explanation: Returns min instead of max.
hint: Check the comparison symbol.

title: Fix off by one
buggy_code: void f(int a[],int n){ for(int i=0;i<=n;i++) a[i]=0; }
correct_code: void f(int a[],int n){ for(int i=0;i<n;i++) a[i]=0; }
test_input: int main(){ int a[2]; f(a,2); printf("%d", a[0]); return 0; }
explanation: Out of bounds <= n.
hint: Check loop condition.

title: Fix null term
buggy_code: void f(char* s, char* d){ int i=0; while(s[i]){ d[i]=s[i]; i++; } }
correct_code: void f(char* s, char* d){ int i=0; while(s[i]){ d[i]=s[i]; i++; } d[i]='\0'; }
test_input: int main(){ char s[]="a", d[2]; f(s,d); printf("%s", d); return 0; }
explanation: Missing null terminator.

title: Fix recursion
buggy_code: int f(int n){ if(n==0) return 1; return n*f(n); }
correct_code: int f(int n){ if(n==0) return 1; return n*f(n-1); }
explanation: Infinite recursion f(n).
hint: Subtract 1 in recursive call.

title: Fix shift
buggy_code: void f(int a[],int n){ for(int i=0;i<n-1;i++) a[i+1]=a[i]; }
correct_code: void f(int a[],int n){ for(int i=n-2;i>=0;i--) a[i+1]=a[i]; }
test_input: int main(){ int a[]={1,2}; f(a,2); printf("%d", a[1]); return 0; }
explanation: Forward shift overwrites.
hint: Shift from end to start.

title: Fix ptr inc
buggy_code: int f(char* s){ int c=0; while(*s){ c++; } return c; }
correct_code: int f(char* s){ int c=0; while(*s){ c++; s++; } return c; }
explanation: Missing s++ in loop.
hint: Move the pointer forward.

title: Fix leap
buggy_code: int f(int y){ if(y%4==0 && y%100!=0) return 1; return 0; }
correct_code: int f(int y){ if(y%400==0) return 1; if(y%100==0) return 0; if(y%4==0) return 1; return 0; }
explanation: Century leap rule error.
hint: Check y%400.

title: Fix bsearch
buggy_code: int f(int a[],int n,int t){ int l=0,r=n-1; while(l<=r){ int m=(l+r)/2; if(a[m]==t) return m; if(a[m]<t) l=m; else r=m-1; } return -1; }
correct_code: int f(int a[],int n,int t){ int l=0,r=n-1; while(l<=r){ int m=(l+r)/2; if(a[m]==t) return m; if(a[m]<t) l=m+1; else r=m-1; } return -1; }
test_input: int main(){ int a[]={1,3}; printf("%d", f(a,2,3)); return 0; }
explanation: l=m causes infinite loop.

title: Fix identity
buggy_code: int f(int m[2][2]){ for(int i=0;i<2;i++) for(int j=0;j<2;j++) if(i==j && m[i][j]==0) return 0; return 1; }
correct_code: int f(int m[2][2]){ for(int i=0;i<2;i++) for(int j=0;j<2;j++) if(i==j && m[i][j]!=1) return 0; else if(i!=j && m[i][j]!=0) return 0; return 1; }
test_input: int main(){ int m[2][2]={{1,0},{0,1}}; printf("%d", f(m)); return 0; }
explanation: Checks only 0s on diag.
hint: Diag must be 1, others 0.

title: Fix bubble
buggy_code: void f(int a[],int n){ for(int i=0;i<n;i++) for(int j=0;j<n;j++) if(a[j]>a[j+1]) {int t=a[j]; a[j]=a[j+1]; a[j+1]=t;} }
correct_code: void f(int a[],int n){ for(int i=0;i<n-1;i++) for(int j=0;j<n-i-1;j++) if(a[j]>a[j+1]) {int t=a[j]; a[j]=a[j+1]; a[j+1]=t;} }
test_input: int main(){ int a[]={2,1}; f(a,2); printf("%d", a[0]); return 0; }
explanation: Out of bounds a[j+1].
hint: Inner loop count n-i-1.

title: Fix dec2bin
buggy_code: long f(int n){ long b=0,i=1; while(n){ b += (n%2)*10; n/=2; i*=10; } return b; }
correct_code: long f(int n){ long b=0,i=1; while(n){ b += (n%2)*i; n/=2; i*=10; } return b; }
explanation: Uses literal 10 instead of power i.
hint: Multiply by placeholder variable i.

title: Fix vector min
buggy_code: int f(vector<int> v){ return *max_element(v.begin(),v.end()); }
correct_code: int f(vector<int> v){ return *min_element(v.begin(),v.end()); }
explanation: Uses max_element.
hint: Use min_element.

title: Fix even count
buggy_code: int f(vector<int> v){ int c=0; for(int x:v) if(x%2!=0) c++; return c; }
correct_code: int f(vector<int> v){ int c=0; for(int x:v) if(x%2==0) c++; return c; }
explanation: Counts odds.

title: Fix suffix
buggy_code: bool f(string s,string b){ return s.substr(s.length()-b.length())!=b; }
correct_code: bool f(string s,string b){ if(b.length()>s.length()) return false; return s.substr(s.length()-b.length())==b; }
explanation: != instead of ==.
hint: Use ==.

title: Fix c2f
buggy_code: float f(float c){ return (c*9/5)-32; }
correct_code: float f(float c){ return (c*9/5)+32; }
explanation: Subtracts 32.
hint: Add 32.

title: Fix same
buggy_code: bool f(int a,int b){ return a=b; }
correct_code: bool f(int a,int b){ return a==b; }
explanation: Assignment instead of comparison.
hint: Use ==.

title: Fix bsearch iter
buggy_code: int f(vector<int> v,int t){ int l=0,r=v.size(); while(l<=r){ int m=l+(r-l)/2; if(v[m]==t) return m; if(v[m]<t) l=m+1; else r=m-1; } return -1; }
correct_code: int f(vector<int> v,int t){ if(v.empty()) return -1; int l=0,r=v.size()-1; while(l<=r){ int m=l+(r-l)/2; if(v[m]==t) return m; if(v[m]<t) l=m+1; else r=m-1; } return -1; }
explanation: r=v.size() causes oob.
hint: r = v.size() - 1.

title: Fix erase-remove
buggy_code: void f(vector<int>& v,int val){ remove(v.begin(),v.end(),val); }
correct_code: void f(vector<int>& v,int val){ v.erase(remove(v.begin(),v.end(),val), v.end()); }
test_input: int main(){ vector<int> v={1,1}; f(v,1); cout << v.size(); return 0; }
explanation: Doesn't call erase().
hint: Use v.erase() with remove().

title: Fix sort check
buggy_code: bool f(vector<int> v){ for(int i=0;i<v.size();i++) if(v[i]>v[i+1]) return 0; return 1; }
correct_code: bool f(vector<int> v){ if(v.size()<2) return 1; for(int i=0;i<v.size()-1;i++) if(v[i]>v[i+1]) return 0; return 1; }
explanation: OOB access v[i+1].
hint: i < v.size() - 1.

title: Fix words count
buggy_code: int f(string s){ stringstream ss(s); string w; int c=1; while(ss>>w) c++; return c; }
correct_code: int f(string s){ stringstream ss(s); string w; int c=0; while(ss>>w) c++; return c; }
test_input: int main(){ cout << f("a b"); return 0; }
explanation: Starts at 1.
hint: Start count at 0.

title: Fix profit
buggy_code: int f(vector<int> v){ if(v.empty()) return 0; int m=v[0],p=0; for(int x:v){ m=min(m,x); p=max(p,m-x); } return p; }
correct_code: int f(vector<int> v){ if(v.empty()) return 0; int m=v[0],p=0; for(int x:v){ m=min(m,x); p=max(p,x-m); } return p; }
explanation: m-p instead of p-m.
hint: x-m gives profit.

title: Fix brackets
buggy_code: bool f(string s){ stack<char> st; for(char c:s){ if(c=='(') st.push(')'); else if(st.empty()||st.top()==c) st.pop(); else return 0;} return st.empty(); }
correct_code: bool f(string s){ stack<char> st; for(char c:s){ if(c=='(') st.push(')'); else if(st.empty()||st.top()!=c) return 0; else st.pop();} return st.empty(); }
explanation: Logic error in pop condition.
hint: Pop only if top matches c.

title: Fix power
buggy_code: double f(double x,int n){ if(n==0) return 1; if(n<0) return 1/f(x,n); double h=f(x,n/2); if(n%2==0) return h*h; return x*h*h; }
correct_code: double f(double x,int n){ if(n==0) return 1; if(n<0) return 1/f(x,-n); double h=f(x,n/2); if(n%2==0) return h*h; return x*h*h; }
explanation: Infinite recursion for negative n.
hint: Negate n in recursive call.

title: Fix str equals
buggy_code: boolean f(String a,String b){ return a == b; }
correct_code: boolean f(String a,String b){ return a.equals(b); }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print(new String("a").equals("a")); }
explanation: Uses == instead of .equals().
hint: Use .equals() for content.

title: Fix array length
buggy_code: int f(int[] a){ return a.length(); }
correct_code: int f(int[] a){ return a.length; }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ int[] a = new int[1]; System.out.print(a.length); }
explanation: Calls length() method on array.
hint: Arrays use .length field.

title: Fix main
buggy_code: public static void main(){ System.out.print("hi"); }
correct_code: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("hi"); }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("hi"); }
explanation: Missing String[] args.
hint: Add parameter to main.

title: Fix abs
buggy_code: int f(int n){ return n < 0 ? n : -n; }
correct_code: int f(int n){ return n < 0 ? -n : n; }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print(5); }
explanation: Inverts abs logic.
hint: If n < 0, return -n.

title: Fix null check
buggy_code: boolean f(String s){ return s.isEmpty() || s == null; }
correct_code: boolean f(String s){ return s == null || s.isEmpty(); }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("true"); }
explanation: Order causes NullPointerException.
hint: Check null before calling isEmpty().

title: Fix list add
buggy_code: List<Integer> f(){ List<Integer> l = new ArrayList<>(); l.add("1"); return l; }
correct_code: List<Integer> f(){ List<Integer> l = new ArrayList<>(); l.add(1); return l; }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("1"); }
explanation: Adds String to Integer list.
hint: Add an integer, not a string.

title: Fix loop bounds
buggy_code: void f(int[] a){ for(int i=1; i<=a.length; i++) a[i]=0; }
correct_code: void f(int[] a){ for(int i=0; i<a.length; i++) a[i]=0; }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("0"); }
explanation: Starts at 1 and goes oob.
hint: Start at 0, i < length.

title: Fix ternary
buggy_code: String f(int s){ return s > 60 ? "Pass" : "Fail"; }
correct_code: String f(int s){ return s >= 60 ? "Pass" : "Fail"; }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("Pass"); }
explanation: Excludes 60 from Pass.

title: Fix const
buggy_code: class A { final int x; A(int v){ x=v; x=10; } }
correct_code: class A { final int x; A(int v){ x=v; } }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("5"); }
explanation: Reassigns final variable.
hint: Assign final only once.

title: Fix stream
buggy_code: long f(List<Integer> l){ return l.stream().filter(x->x>0).count(); }
correct_code: long f(List<Integer> l){ return l.stream().filter(x->x>0).mapToLong(x->x).sum(); }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("3"); }
explanation: Counts instead of summing.
hint: Use sum().

title: Fix singleton
buggy_code: class S { static S i; S get(){ if(i==null) i=new S(); return i; } }
correct_code: class S { static S i; static S get(){ if(i==null) i=new S(); return i; } }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("true"); }
explanation: Method get() not static.
hint: Make getter static.

title: Fix compareTo
buggy_code: int f(int a,int b){ return a-b; }
correct_code: int f(int a,int b){ return Integer.compare(a, b); }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("true"); }
explanation: Subtraction can overflow.
hint: Use Integer.compare().

title: Fix division
buggy_code: double f(int a,int b){ return a/b; }
correct_code: double f(int a,int b){ return (double)a/b; }
test_input: public static void main(String[] args){ System.out.print("2.5"); }
explanation: Integer division loses precision.
hint: Cast to double.
